key_insight: The paper extends large-step gradient descent theory from isolated flat minima to manifolds of flat minima and to vector-valued least-squares, while preserving a normal form and convergence guarantees.
engineering_relevance: It provides a more realistic convergence framework for overparameterized optimization problems such as deep matrix factorization and regression where standard step-size conditions are often violated.
method: Generalize the large-step GD normal form to vector-valued outputs and manifolds of flat minima.
Prove three convergence theorems in the broader setting by handling a singular PDE with a new solution method.
Apply the framework to deep matrix factorization and derive geometric structure of the flat-minimum set.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical extension and proof of new structural results, but does not mention empirical experiments or benchmark evaluation.
watch_item: The results rely on specific local geometric assumptions near a flat-minimum manifold, so practical use may depend on verifying those conditions in a given model.
paper_title: Dynamics of Gradient Descent with Large Step Size Near a Manifold of Flat Minima
